title: Application of biomolecular techniques on tsetse fly puparia for species identification at larvipostion sites
author: Boulangé, Alain
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The study explores the use of biomolecular techniques to identify tsetse fly species from their empty puparia, the hardened shells left behind after larvae pupate. Five DNA extraction methods were compared, with Chelex, CTAB, and DEB+K proving most effective for fresh puparia. For older puparia, Chelex was the most reliable, allowing identification up to seven months after pupation. By applying the Chelex method to field-collected puparia, the researchers successfully identified 60% of the samples at the species level. This technique opens up new possibilities for studying tsetse fly ecology and behavior by analyzing their pupation sites.
